title: What She Left Behind
timeline: Autumn 2126, three weeks after Adaeze's death
pov_character: Kirin Saleh (primary); brief close-third shifts to Perun and Brigid
summary: Kirin spends one long day in Sable River tracing what her grandmother left behind. Perun, the archivist, reveals he was a Rootstock witness himself in 2078 and knew Adaeze by another name: Adaeze Okonkwo. Her supervisor Brigid recognizes the leaving before Kirin names it and releases her with grace. Tomas outfits her with maps and practical advice. A dinner with friends surfaces the Compact's casual blindness about the depopulated zones. At an old-timers' club called The Bench, Kirin meets Reva, a woman who knew Adaeze in a plateau settlement in 2081 — Rootstock, Tamsat-writing, founded by a woman named Nnedi. The turn comes when a Ghost-class elder-care aide, Mira, tells Kirin about a song Adaeze used to sing to a dying man in an unknown language, and Kirin understands that her grandmother was not hiding — she was on watch. She packs, leaves notes, stops at Adaeze's empty apartment at dawn, and goes east.
locations: The Bench (old-timers' social club, Quarry Street)
Sable River elder-care facility (west civic district)
themes: paradigm blindness and the casual nod
what home means when you are leaving it
continuity_notes: Adaeze's pre-Compact name is now canon: Adaeze Okonkwo. The unreadable letters are in Tamsat, a reconstructed language used by some Rootstock communities. Perun was a Rootstock records specialist and one of the three witnesses on Adaeze's first claim registration in 2078. The plateau settlement Kirin is heading to was founded by a woman named Nnedi and was mixed African diaspora. The key's note 'Be patient with what it asks of you' is reinterpreted in Ep2 as a handoff of a thirty-five-year watch. Mira, the Ghost-class aide, mentioned that an elderly resident named Efe stopped eating the day after Adaeze died — potential future thread. Amira is a wind-turbine specialist who lived in the eastern reach; she gave Kirin a short-range point-to-point comm, solar cells, and a water filter. Tomas gave Kirin infrastructure maps and warned her about reservoir water. Brigid flagged Kirin's station as 'Held at supervisor discretion' — note says she's used that three times, all three returned. Ep3 should open with Kirin already outside Sable River, crossing the frontier.
